title: Validate cron ids before shell interpolation
description: Enforce safe, basename-matched cron IDs before scheduling or shell wrapper generation.
selectionRationale: Research selection: queue was empty; /harness-audit ranked unvalidated cron id interpolation as the top fresh high-impact harness-infra finding. Filed as #128.
acceptanceCriteria: Cron ids are accepted only when they match ^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$.
Explicit frontmatter id values must match the cron file basename; omitted ids still derive from the basename.
Invalid or mismatched cron files are skipped and logged without scheduling or crashing the runtime.
Tests cover valid ids, invalid characters/shell metacharacters, path traversal-like ids, basename mismatch, and scheduleAll skip accounting.
crons/README.md documents that runtime enforcement exists and names the new status tokens.
